rename.from: Replace `regex` dep with stdlib `re` to avoid Windows build-from-source failures
rename.to: Tighten requires-python upper bound to <3.14 until regex ships stable wheels

issue.body:
## Problem

`requires-python = ">=3.10,<4.0"` allows uv to select Python 3.14 (currently pre-release/alpha). The `regex` dependency does not yet ship pre-built wheels for 3.14, so uv falls back to a source build. On Windows that fails without MSVC Build Tools:

```
error: Microsoft Visual C++ 14.0 or greater is required.
```

The installer prints "Done!" anyway, leaving users with a silently broken install.

## Fix

Tighten to `requires-python = ">=3.10,<3.14"` until Python 3.14 reaches stable release and `regex` publishes matching wheels.

Once that happens, bump the upper bound back to `<4.0` (or whatever the next ceiling makes sense).

## Workaround (for users hitting this now)

```powershell
uv tool install --python 3.13 --reinstall "pd-ocr-cli @ git+https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-ocr-cli"
```

nodes.body:
## Short-term mitigation: tighten `requires-python`

Current: `requires-python = ">=3.10,<4.0"` — this allows uv to select Python 3.14 (pre-release), which has no pre-built `regex` wheels yet.

Tightening to `>=3.10,<3.14` in `pyproject.toml` would prevent uv from ever resolving 3.14 for this package or anything that depends on it, closing the Windows compiler error without waiting for the `regex` replacement.

This is a stopgap — bump the upper bound once 3.14 goes stable and the dependency wheel ecosystem catches up (or once `regex` is replaced with stdlib `re`, at which point the cap can be removed entirely).

issue.title: Tighten requires-python upper bound to <3.14 until regex ships stable wheels

issue.body:
## Problem

`requires-python = ">=3.13,<4.0"` allows uv to select Python 3.14 (currently pre-release/alpha). Transitive dependency `regex` (via `pd-book-tools`) does not yet ship pre-built wheels for 3.14, so uv falls back to a source build. On Windows that fails without MSVC Build Tools.

## Fix

Change to `requires-python = ">=3.13,<3.14"`. Revert to `<4.0` once Python 3.14 is stable and `regex` publishes matching wheels.

Tracked upstream: https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools/issues/23

issue.body:
## Problem

`requires-python = ">=3.13"` has no upper bound, allowing uv to select Python 3.14 (currently pre-release/alpha). Transitive dependency `regex` (via `pd-book-tools`) does not yet ship pre-built wheels for 3.14, so uv falls back to a source build. On Windows that fails without MSVC Build Tools.

## Fix

Change to `requires-python = ">=3.13,<3.14"`. Revert to removing the cap once Python 3.14 is stable and `regex` publishes matching wheels.

Tracked upstream: https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools/issues/23

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #23

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#316

Evidence:

- `pyproject.toml:43`
- `pyproject.toml:85`

`python-doctr` is redirected to a Git URL without explicit revision in `pyproject.toml`. The lock pins a commit today, but `uv lock --upgrade` can move it to arbitrary upstream code.

Impact:

- Dependency upgrades can pull unreviewed source outside normal release-version review.

Remediation:

- Use a released `python-doctr` version if possible.
- Otherwise pin the Git source to an immutable tag/commit in `pyproject.toml` and document upgrade procedure.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: floating Git dependency weakens reproducibility
